name: Isfahan Bazaar & Imam Square Walking Tour
name_fa: تور پیاده‌روی بازار و میدان امام اصفهان

stops.description: Meet your guide at the north entrance. Overview of the square layout and history.
Admire the turquoise tilework of this Safavid masterpiece from the exterior courtyard.
Ascend to the music room and enjoy panoramic views across the square.
Enter the main bazaar gate and explore carpet, spice, and silversmith sections.
Step inside for the famous dome optical illusion—no minaret, private royal access.
Rest with Persian tea and gaz (nougat) overlooking the square.

name: Shiraz Old City & Poets' Quarter Walking Tour
name_fa: تور پیاده‌روی شهر قدیم و محله شعرا شیراز

stops.description: Begin at the restored Zand-era bathhouse, now a museum of mannequins depicting traditional life.
Walk through 275-year-old vaulted brick corridors lined with spice, carpet, and jewelry merchants.
Enter the mosque famous for its twisted stone columns and intricate tile mihrabs.
Stroll past the citadel exterior and explore the Zand district square.
Visit the alabaster tomb of Persia's greatest lyric poet in the rose garden.
End at the poet Saadi's tranquil mausoleum surrounded by cypresses and a reflecting pool.

name: Yazd Wind-Towers & Old Fabric Walking Tour
name_fa: تور پیاده‌روی بادگیرها و بافت قدیم یزد

stops.description: Start at the iconic triple-storey facade; enjoy the square before the crowds arrive.
Descend into a Qanat-fed cistern to understand Yazd's ancient water engineering.
Walk to the tallest minarets in Iran and study the intricate tiling of the Timurid portal.
See the 33-metre wind-tower — the world's tallest — cooling the garden's central pool.
Visit the Zoroastrian sacred fire burning continuously since 470 CE.
Refresh with dates and herb tea on a traditional rooftop cafe with panoramic views.

name: Kashan Historic Houses & Rose-Water District Walking Tour
name_fa: تور پیاده‌روی خانه‌های تاریخی و منطقه گلاب کاشان

stops.description: Walk through Iran's oldest surviving formal garden; hear the story of Amir Kabir's assassination.
Explore four courtyards of the grandest merchant mansion in Kashan.
Admire the 18-metre wind-catcher and the intricate stucco portrait frieze.
View the domed hammam with its star-shaped skylights, beautifully restored.
Walk the carpet and copper sections; pick up saffron or rosewater as a souvenir.

name: Tehran Darband & Northern Hills Walking Tour
name_fa: تور پیاده‌روی دربند و ارتفاعات شمالی تهران

stops.description: Meet at the square and walk the initial paved street up to the gorge entrance.
Pass the first cluster of stream-side cafes; optional light breakfast.
Rest at the flat stone ledge with views south across the sprawling city.
Optional 30-minute extension to the rock climbing crags for great mountain views.
Return trail with views of Tochal peak (3,964 m) to the west.
Browse fresh herbs, pickles, and sweets; visit the Imamzadeh Saleh shrine.

name: Tehran Street Food & Southern Bazaar Food Tour
name_fa: تور غذای خیابانی و بازار جنوب تهران

stops.description: Meet at the bazaar entrance; guide explains the food geography of south Tehran.
Taste Iran's oldest dish: lamb, chickpea and potato stew mashed tableside.
Sample herb-and-kidney-bean stew from a kitchen that hasn't changed its recipe in 90 years.

stops.place: Spice alley of the Grand Bazaar
stops.description: Smell and taste saffron, dried limes, barberries, and sumac with the merchant's commentary.
Hot sangak flatbread from a stone-bed oven, sohan sesame brittle, and baklava.
Guide prepares ash-e reshteh while you look out over the Grand Bazaar domes.

name: Isfahan Traditional Cuisine & Madar-e Shah Caravanserai Food Tour
name_fa: تور غذای سنتی اصفهان و کاروانسرای مادر شاه

stops.description: Start with cardamom-spiced chai and qottab almond pastries.
Taste Isfahan's signature lamb-offal flatbread wrap, a 300-year-old recipe.
Sit-down fesenjan and joojeh kabab with saffron rice.
Watch a tahdig demonstration: how to achieve the perfect golden rice crust.
Finish with rosewater sorbet noodles and saffron ice cream.

name: Tabriz Grand Bazaar & Azerbaijani Table Food Tour
name_fa: تور غذایی بازار بزرگ تبریز و سفره آذربایجانی

stops.description: Enter the bazaar and orient with a short history of its 1,000-year trading role.
Taste pistachios, mulberries, barberries and learn how to shop like a local.
Meet a spice blender who creates custom mixes for the regional stew repertoire.
Sit down to kufteh Tabrizi, ash-e doogh (yogurt soup), and tahdig.
Watch saffron halva being poured and taste warm from the tray.
Roll Tabriz-style reshteh pasta by hand and take home your efforts.

name: Rasht Caspian Kitchen & Gilaki Market Food Tour
name_fa: تور غذایی آشپزخانه کاسپین و بازار گیلکی رشت

stops.description: Navigate Rasht's chaotic market for spring onions, herbs, salted fish, and pickles.
Taste Caspian smoked carp and white fish; learn how they are cold-smoked over rice straw.
Hands-on session making mirza ghasemi: charring eggplant, folding in eggs and garlic.
Sit down to baghali ghatogh, Gilaki rice, pickled garlic, and smoked fish.
Finish with lavashak (sour fruit leather) and fresh pomegranate juice.

name: Isfahan Armenian Quarter & Vank Cathedral Cultural Tour
name_fa: تور فرهنگی محله ارامنه و کلیسای وانک اصفهان

stops.description: Admire the blended Safavid-Armenian architecture before the doors open.
Guided explanation of the biblical scenes painted by 17th-century Armenian artists.
Small but powerful collection of documents and artefacts; respectful discussion.
Watch gata pastry being rolled and taste fresh from the oven.
Tea and conversation with a host family about daily life as Christians in Iran.

name: Shiraz Persian Gardens & Sufi Shrine Cultural Tour
name_fa: تور فرهنگی باغ‌های ایرانی و خانقاه صوفیان شیراز

stops.description: Iran's most celebrated UNESCO Persian garden; stroll among ancient cypresses and orange trees.
Enter the 19th-century Qajar mansion and its orange-blossom garden.
Visit the mirror-mosaic mausoleum of Ahmad ibn Musa (non-Muslim visitors welcome with head covering).
Traditional Persian lunch in one of Shiraz's landmark restaurants.
Attend a sama ceremony and speak with the sheikh about Sufi philosophy.

name: Persepolis Day Trip from Isfahan — Cultural Heritage Tour
name_fa: تور میراث فرهنگی تخت جمشید — یک‌روزه از اصفهان

stops.description: Depart early to arrive before the heat and tour groups.
Enter through the lamassu-guarded gateway; overview of the site plan.
Study the delegation friezes: 28 nations represented in stone.
Explore the largest hall of the complex; understand military and administrative function.
Traditional lunch at a local restaurant in Marvdasht.
Four royal tombs carved into a cliff; Sassanid victory reliefs below.
Stand before the simple limestone tomb of the founder of the Persian Empire.
Arrive back at Isfahan hotels.

name: Yazd Zoroastrian Heritage & Tower of Silence Cultural Tour
name_fa: تور میراث زرتشتی و دخمه یزد

stops.description: View the sacred flame burning since 470 CE through the temple window; explanation of fire symbolism.
Hike to the two hilltop circular platforms used until the 1970s for sky burials.
Meet community members; hear first-hand accounts of faith and identity.
Shared meal with a Zoroastrian host family.
Watch artisans making sudreh (sacred shirt) and kusti cord worn by the faithful.
Panoramic view over the desert city; closing discussion on Iran's religious diversity.

name: Persepolis & Naqsh-e Rostam Full-Day Historical Tour from Shiraz
name_fa: تور تاریخی تخت جمشید و نقش رستم — یک‌روزه از شیراز

stops.description: Air-conditioned minibus departs for the 60 km drive north.
Context lecture on the Achaemenid Empire before entering.
Deep study of the eastern and northern staircase reliefs.
Explore the Palace of Darius, Xerxes, and the 100-Column Hall.
Simple local lunch with lamb stew and flatbread.
Four royal tombs of Darius I, Xerxes, Artaxerxes I, and Darius II.
Sassanid investiture reliefs 2 km south.
Drop-off at hotels by early evening.

name: Isfahan Safavid Dynasty Historical Deep-Dive Tour
name_fa: تور تاریخ عمیق دوره صفوی اصفهان

stops.description: The square as a political and commercial stage; role of urban design in Safavid statecraft.
Royal reception hall, throne room, and the music room with plaster vessel alcoves.
Theological significance of its angled entry: reconciling city grid with Mecca alignment.
Safavid frescoes of battles, banquets, and diplomatic receptions; hidden symbolism.
The Eight Paradises palace garden; remaining tilework and iwan.
Lunch at a historic caravanserai with a continuation of the historical narrative.

name: Taq-e Bostan & Kermanshah Ancient Reliefs Day Trip from Tehran
name_fa: تور طاق بستان و نقوش برجسته باستانی کرمانشاه از تهران

stops.description: Early flight to Kermanshah (1 hour).
Relief of Ardashir II investiture; earlier Sassanid style.
Khusraw II on armored horse Shabdiz; royal hunt scenes in upper panels.
En route discuss Achaemenid versus Sassanid artistic traditions.
Darius I's trilingual proclamation 100 metres up the cliff face; Henry Rawlinson's 1835 copy.
Local khorak and lamb kebab.
Transfer to Kermanshah airport for evening flight.

name: Hamadan — Tomb of Avicenna & Ancient Ecbatana Historical Tour
name_fa: تور تاریخی همدان — آرامگاه ابن سینا و اکباتان باستانی

stops.description: Depart early for the 340 km drive (4 hours) to Hamadan.
The 1950s modernist tower houses the tomb of Ibn Sina (980–1037 CE); museum of his medical manuscripts.
2,500-year-old Median lion sculpture at the base of Hegmataneh hill.
Excavated Median and Achaemenid settlement; on-site guide explains stratigraphy.
Hamadan's signature dish: ab doogh khiar cold yogurt soup and lamb kebab.
Biblical-era tomb; speak with the Jewish custodian about the community's 2,700-year presence in Iran.
Optional: the world's largest water cave, 60 km north; boat tour inside.
Arrive back in Tehran.

name: Mesr Desert Overnight Camel Trek
name_fa: کاروان شتری و اقامت شبانه کویر مصر

stops.description: Meet guides and camels; load saddlebags with overnight gear.
2-hour ride across the erg to the overnight camp.
Erect traditional tents; explore the dune crests for sunset views.
Lamb kebab, flatbread, salad, and desert tea under the stars.
Guide identifies constellations used by desert navigators; zero light pollution.
Climb the highest dune for the color-shift of sunrise across the sand sea.
Light breakfast; camel ride back to Khur by 09:00.

name: Varzaneh Salt Lake & White Desert Photography Safari
name_fa: سافاری عکاسی دریاچه نمک و کویر سفید ورزنه

stops.description: Pre-dawn departure in 4x4 vehicles.
Drive onto the flat; photograph the mirror reflections in the salt crust.
Seasonal flamingo colony on the lake margins; bird and wildlife spotting.
Dune-boarding and photography on the 30-metre crescent dunes.
Traditional lunch in a restored caravanserai courtyard.
Round towers built to collect pigeon dung for fertilizer; unique desert architecture.
Arrive back by early evening.

name: Maranjab Desert Jeep & Camping Adventure
name_fa: ماجراجویی جیپ و کمپینگ کویر مرنجاب

stops.description: Load equipment into 4x4 jeeps; safety and desert briefing.

stops.place: Aran o Bidgol salt lake edge
stops.description: First stop: vast white salt expanse with Alborz mountains visible to the north.
Explore the Safavid waystation on the historic Silk Road; rooftop sunset view.
Pitch insulated tents; guide prepares fireside cooking.
Lamb and vegetable stew; stories of Silk Road caravans and desert survival.
Long-exposure shooting of the Milky Way with equipment guidance.
Walk the cracked salt surface at sunrise before returning to Kashan by 09:00.

name: Yazd Rooftop & Golden Hour Photography Workshop
name_fa: کارگاه عکاسی بام‌ها و ساعت طلایی یزد

stops.description: Pre-dawn start; guide reviews camera settings for low-light mud-brick scenes.
Shoot the iconic wind-catcher skyline in the blue hour before sunrise.
Frame the Jame Mosque minarets against changing sky colors.
Narrow passages where morning sun creates dramatic directional light beams.
Wide landscape shots of the entire old city.
Lightroom tips over tea and flatbread; review the morning's work.

name: Isfahan Bridges at Dusk Photography Tour
name_fa: تور عکاسی پل‌های اصفهان در غروب

stops.description: Arrive 90 minutes before sunset for the warm-light arch reflections.
Capture locals sipping tea in the atmospheric arch niches.
Cross to the east side for the sun-behind-bridge silhouette shot.
Tripod long-exposure of the illuminated arches reflected in the river.
The oldest bridge in Isfahan (Sassanid foundations); atmospheric and tourist-free.

name: Hormozgan Qeshm Island & Mangrove Photography Expedition
name_fa: اکسپدیشن عکاسی جزیره قشم و جنگل مانگرو هرمزگان

stops.description: Fly or ferry from Bandar Abbas; settle into guest house; evening briefing.
Shoot the eroded limestone canyon in golden hour light.
Local fishing boat into the mangrove channels before sunrise.
Flamingo colony photography from shallow-draft skiff.
Shoot the old Portuguese-era wind-towers and harbor at dusk.
Dramatic narrow canyon with freshwater spring; post-processed at breakfast.
Return ferry or flight from Qeshm.

name: Classic Iran 7-Day Tour: Tehran–Kashan–Isfahan–Yazd–Shiraz
name_fa: تور ۷ روزه کلاسیک ایران: تهران–کاشان–اصفهان–یزد–شیراز

stops.description: National Museum, Treasury of National Jewels, Grand Bazaar, Golestan Palace.
Drive south; Tabatabaei and Borujerdi Houses, Fin Garden, Bazaar.
Drive to Isfahan; Armenian Julfa quarter, Vank Cathedral, riverside walk.
Full day: Imam Square, Shah Mosque, Ali Qapu, Chehel Sotoun, Si-o-se Pol at dusk.
Drive through central Iran; Yazd old city, Jame Mosque, Dolat Abad, wind-towers.
Tower of Silence, Zoroastrian fire temple, drive to Shiraz; Hafez tomb at night.
Full morning at Persepolis and Naqsh-e Rostam; afternoon Vakil Bazaar; flight home.

name: Silk Road Iran: 10-Day Tour Tehran–Tabriz–Zanjan–Hamadan–Kermanshah
name_fa: تور ۱۰ روزه جاده ابریشم ایران

stops.description: National Museum, Tehran Bazaar, Sa'dabad Palace complex.
Grand Bazaar (UNESCO), Blue Mosque, Kandovan cliff village day trip.
UNESCO Soltaniyeh dome (14th century); world's third-largest masonry dome.
Avicenna Mausoleum, Hegmataneh excavations, stone lion, Esther Tomb.
Taq-e Bostan, Bisotun UNESCO inscription, Zagros village homestay.
Scenic drive back through Zagros mountains; evening flight connections.

name: Northern Iran Caspian Circuit: 5-Day Tour Rasht–Gilan–Talesh–Masuleh
name_fa: تور ۵ روزه مدار شمالی ایران: رشت–گیلان–طالش–ماسوله

stops.description: Drive through Alborz; arrive in Rasht; market tour and Gilaki dinner.
Rasht food tour; rice paddy walk; UNESCO Creative Gastronomy city.
Morning in the car-free hillside village; afternoon castle trek through forest.
Off-road into the Talesh highlands; traditional Gilaki village homestay.
Boat through the UNESCO-candidate wetland; birdwatching; return to Tehran.

name: Qashqai Nomad Migration Experience — Fars Province
name_fa: تجربه کوچ عشایر قشقایی — استان فارس

stops.description: Drive 3 hours to meet the family at their winter camp; introductions and camp setup.
Walk with the flock through scrub hills; guide translates conversations with the khan.
Help set up the black tent; watch milking; yogurt-making demonstration.
The longest walking day: 20–25 km through Zagros mountain passes.
Women demonstrate Qashqai kilim-weaving patterns; fire stories and poetry.
Summit crossing with views over the Fars plain; ceremonial arrival.
4x4 vehicle returns travelers to Shiraz.

name: Bakhtiari Nomad Homestay — Chaharmahal va Bakhtiari
name_fa: اقامت نزد عشایر بختیاری — چهارمحال و بختیاری

stops.description: 3-hour drive into the Zagros; arrive at camp by early afternoon.
Learn tent structure, daily schedule, and basic Bakhtiari phrases.
Walk with the shepherd to bring the flock back to camp before dusk.
Share lamb and herb stew; traditional flute and hand-drum session.
Assist with milking, doogh preparation, and rug-weaving; optional mountain walk.
Say farewell; return to Isfahan by early afternoon.

name: Alamut Valley & Assassin Castles Trekking Adventure
name_fa: ماجراجویی کوهنوردی دره الموت و قلعه‌های حشاشین

stops.description: Drive 2 hours to Qazvin then 1 hour on mountain road into Alamut Valley.
Steep 45-minute ascent to the rock fortress of Hassan-i Sabbah; spectacular valley views.
Settle into local family home; evening discussion of Ismaili history.
6-hour ridge walk at 2,500 m from Alamut to the Lambsar approach.
Second great Assassin fortress; less visited and more atmospheric.
Homestay in a Caspian-slope village; local dialect, fresh walnut harvest.
Trail drops steeply through oak and hornbeam forest to the valley floor; return to Tehran.

name: Tehran to Qazvin & Alamut Valley Day Trip
name_fa: یک‌روزه تهران به قزوین و دره الموت

stops.description: Depart in a comfortable minibus heading northwest on the Qazvin motorway.
Safavid-era garden palace with painted pavilion; pre-Isfahan capital of Iran.
Quick walk through the covered market; sample local confections.
Dramatic mountain road following the Shah Rud river gorge.
Lamb stew and flatbread in a tea house with walnut-orchard views.
Walk to the castle gate (30-minute ascent) or enjoy views from the road.
Arrive back by 17:30.
